The EPR Registration Certificate of M/S ACER INDIA PRIVATE LTD  shall be valid for
a period of five (5) years from date of issue with following conditions:
i.
You shall create awareness through media, publications, advertisements, posters or
by any other means of communication;
ii.
You shall file annual returns in the laid down form on the portal on or before 30th day
of June following the financial year to which that returns relates;
iii.
The producer shall fulfil their extended producer responsibility obligations through
online purchase of extended producer responsibility certificate from only those
recyclers having obtained recycler registration certificate from CPCB through the EPR
portal and whose name appears as registered recyclers on the EPR portal
themselves and submit it online by filing quarterly return;
5.
General Terms & Conditions of the Authorisation:
a.
The producer  shall comply with provisions of the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986
and the E-waste (Management) Rules, 2022 made there under;
b.
An application for the renewal of registration shall be made before 120 days of expiry
of this EPR Registration Certificate
c.
The Board reserves right to cancel/amend/revoke the registration at any time as per
the policy.
6.

iii. You shall ensure that all the collected e-waste is channelized to your
recycler/dismantler M/s Exigo Recycling Pvt Ltd, M/s SIMS Recycling, M/s
Resource E-Waste and M/s Attero Recycling Pvt Ltd and records shall be
maintained at dismantler/recycler and at your end;
iv. You shall maintain records, in Form-2 of these Rules, of e-waste and make such
records available for scrutiny by Central Pollution Control Board;
v. You shall file annual returns in Form-3 to the Central Pollution Control Board on or
before 30th day of June following the financial year to which that returns relates.
vi.   General Terms & Conditions of the Authorisation:
a.The authorisation shall comply with provisions of the Environment (Protection) Act,
1986 and the E-waste (Management) Rules,2016 made there under;
b.The authorisation or its renewal shall be produced for inspection at the request of
an officer authorised by the Central Pollution Control Board;
c.Any change in the approved Extended Producer Responsibility plan should be
informed to Central Pollution Control Board within 15 days on which decision shall
be communicated by Central Pollution Control Board within sixty days;
d.It is the duty of the authorised person to take prior permission of the Central
Pollution Control Board to close down any collection centre/points or any other
facility which are part of the EPR plan;
e.An application for the renewal of authorisation shall be made as laid down in sub-
rule (vi) of rule of 13(1) the E- Waste (Management) Rules, 2016;
f.The Board reserves right to cancel/amend/revoke the authorisation at any time as
per the policy of the Board or Government.
vii.   Additional Conditions: -
a)That the applicant will submit annual sales data along with annual returns;
b)That the applicant has to ensure that the addresses of collection points provided
by them in their EPR Plan are correct and traceable and the collection
points/centres are functional;
c)That the applicant will submit revised application for grant of EPR Authorisation in
case of applicant adding/changing PRO or changing its EPR Plan;

WHEREAS:
 
a)  Acer is engaged in the manufacture/import of desktop computer systems, servers, laptops, notebooks,
monitors, projectors, tablet PCs and supporting IT peripherals (hereinafter referred to as Products), and
trading thereof, and provision of ancillary services connected therewith.
b)  The ASP is engaged inter alia in providing repair services for electronic equipment.
c)  The Products are covered under Acers limited product warranty policy. Warranty support services shall
be provided by Acer to the end customers through the ASP, so long as the Products are covered under
warranty as well as the regular support services for the Products, while they are not covered under Acers
limited product warranty policy. 
d)  Acer offers to appoint the ASP for providing warranty support services for Acers Products, to the
Customers, on behalf of Acer as set out in Schedule A hereto (hereinafter referred to as Services) and the
ASP agrees to accept such proposal and to provide the Services.
 
NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the premises and mutual covenants set forth herein below and
for good and other valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby duly
acknowledged, the Parties hereto covenant and agree as follows:
 
1.	SCOPE OF WORK
 
1.1.	Acer appoints ASP for providing the warranty support services and the ASP accept such appointment
and agrees to provide Services, within the territories assigned by Acer from time to time, for and on behalf
of Acer, in accordance with the terms and conditions as hereinbelow. Such warranty support services are
mentioned and set out in Schedule A hereto (hereinafter referred to as “Services”).
1.2.	The ASP hereby acknowledges that Acer reserves the right, during the term of this Agreement and
thereafter, to enter into other arrangements either directly or through independent agents or other
appointed service providers or third parties, without any obligation or liability to the ASP for providing the
Services or any part thereof within the territory assigned by Acer.
1.3.	The ASP shall not attempt to repair any defective electronic components, parts and/or spares of the
Products at its workshop/repair centre/laboratory located at its premises or elsewhere wherein it exercises
its control or through the sub-contractors. It shall only replace them by obtaining the suitable and/or
equivalent electronic components, parts and/or spares from Acer. 
 
 
2.  AGREEMENT PERIOD
 
2.1.    This Agreement shall be effective from 15 Mar 2024 hereinafter referred to as Effective Date) and

--- Page 16 ---
shall be remain valid till 31st December 2024 (hereinafter referred to as Term), unless it is terminated
earlier as hereinafter provided in this Agreement.
2.2.    Acer shall have the option to extend the Term of this Agreement on certain terms and conditions
mutually agreed between the Parties in this Agreement, for a further period of one (01) year. Acer shall
extend the term of this Agreement by notifying the ASP in writing about its intention to extend the period,

 Customer Induced defect/DAMAGE (CID) Document
 
FAQ on CID for Service provider
 
Q1. What is CID?
Ans.    Customer Induced Defect/Damage observed due to PCB Track cut or scratched/ PCB
Broken/Component missing/ CPU socket pin bent/ PCB or Component Burnt/ Serial Number Label
tempered.
Q2. Which all cases Qualify for CID?
Ans.    All the above mentioned cases will qualify for CID.
Q3. Does a CID case qualify for a warranty replacement?
Ans.    CID is considered as customer induced and is not covered by any standard warranty. Therefore, it
will be the Customer's responsibility to cover the cost of spare unless they have prior approval from the
AIL Customer Support Manager.
Q4. At what stage CID case is identified by Acer & what would be the action?
Ans.    There will be 2 stages, 1) physical verification of a defective part for visual damage when received
at Warehouse, while making a receiver in System & 2) At the Repair Centre in Dhaka, which will primarily
highlight cases of component missing, burnt and track cut. AIL will bill such CID Spares to respective
Service provider as per AIL price list with additional cost USD 5 towards logistics. CID spares returned to
Service along with invoice. In case of any exception/ waiver - the necessary mail approval from the
Customer Support Manager needs to be provided within 3 BDs from the date of intimation.
Q5. If in the rare case, part received from Acer is found to be Damaged, what needs to be done?

--- Page 34 ---
Ans.    Service provider has to physically verify each & every part received from Acer and if any part found
to be damaged, same needs to be reported to Acer Branch Customer Support Manager with in 24 Hrs
(Next working day) of receipt by an Email, failing which if service provider returns the parts as unused /
received DOA after a period & if its identified as CID same will attract Penalty.
Q6. What should be done to avoid damages in transit which results as CID ?
Ans.    Service Provider should return the defective part in the same box against which the good spare
was received from Acer . Packing plays a very vital role in avoiding transit damage which will be seen as
CID case, if due care is not taken. In case Partner feels the quality of the box/packing is not up to the
mark, they should inform Acer for sending the packing material/Box.
Q7. In the event of attending a call, Engineer finds the customer part to be CID, what should
   be done?
Ans.    A)  Customer needs to be informed on the spot. Usually burnt case of Adapters, SMPS
           , M/b and TFT monitors etc. are due to the site conditions which are not suitable for proper
           functioning of the electronic devices.
           In case of doubt, must speak to the Branch CSM/ Regional Technical Support Manager by
           providing the Snap shot with the Case ID & FRU SL#.
 
Service providers CID case processing:-
 
In the event of getting a part which falls under CID category, under mentioned process
would be followed:-
 
An e-mail communication from HO will be sent to respective Branch CSM, who in turn will
further inform their respective SERVICE PROVIDER  s with CID details & snapshot of the affected part.
 
Declaration of CID case:- From Warehouse, within 3 days of the material received date and
from Repair Centre- Dhaka, within 10 days from the material received date.
If SERVICE PROVIDER   desires, he can visit the Warehouse within 2 BDs of getting the intimation to
verify the defective part. Acer HO will wait for 3 BDs for the Branch to revert in case of any exception/
waiver that Branch CSM may want with the approval of RCSM. Once the confirmation is received,
thereafter a communication will be sent to Payout processing team for initiating the deduction.
Any query or clarification sought should be taken up with your respective RCSM.
The Penalty amount will be deducted (as per the Table given in the next page ) from the
current Partners claim/ payout & the details of the Partner invoice will be e-mailed to the
respective Branch for necessary reference.
In the event of pre-approved CID case, SERVICE PROVIDER   to put the e-mail approval printout along
with the affected part.

 Customer Induced defect/DAMAGE (CID) Document
 
FAQ on CID for Service provider
 
Q1. What is CID?
Ans.    Customer Induced Defect/Damage observed due to PCB Track cut or scratched/ PCB
Broken/Component missing/ CPU socket pin bent/ PCB or Component Burnt/ Serial Number Label
tempered.
Q2. Which all cases Qualify for CID?
Ans.    All the above mentioned cases will qualify for CID.
Q3. Does a CID case qualify for a warranty replacement?
Ans.    CID is considered as customer induced and is not covered by any standard warranty. Therefore, it
will be the Customer's responsibility to cover the cost of spare unless they have prior approval from the
AIL Customer Support Manager.
Q4. At what stage CID case is identified by Acer & what would be the action?
Ans.    There will be 2 stages, 1) physical verification of a defective part for visual damage when received
at Warehouse, while making a receiver in System & 2) At the Repair Centre in Dhaka, which will primarily
highlight cases of component missing, burnt and track cut. AIL will bill such CID Spares to respective
Service provider as per AIL price list with additional cost USD 5 towards logistics. CID spares returned to
Service along with invoice. In case of any exception/ waiver - the necessary mail approval from the
Customer Support Manager needs to be provided within 3 BDs from the date of intimation.
Q5. If in the rare case, part received from Acer is found to be Damaged, what needs to be done?

--- Page 63 ---
Ans.    Service provider has to physically verify each & every part received from Acer and if any part found
to be damaged, same needs to be reported to Acer Branch Customer Support Manager with in 24 Hrs
(Next working day) of receipt by an Email, failing which if service provider returns the parts as unused /
received DOA after a period & if its identified as CID same will attract Penalty.
Q6. What should be done to avoid damages in transit which results as CID ?
Ans.    Service Provider should return the defective part in the same box against which the good spare
was received from Acer . Packing plays a very vital role in avoiding transit damage which will be seen as
CID case, if due care is not taken. In case Partner feels the quality of the box/packing is not up to the
mark, they should inform Acer for sending the packing material/Box.
Q7. In the event of attending a call, Engineer finds the customer part to be CID, what should
   be done?
Ans.    A)  Customer needs to be informed on the spot. Usually burnt case of Adapters, SMPS
           , M/b and TFT monitors etc. are due to the site conditions which are not suitable for proper
           functioning of the electronic devices.
           In case of doubt, must speak to the Branch CSM/ Regional Technical Support Manager by
           providing the Snap shot with the Case ID & FRU SL#.
 
Service providers CID case processing:-
 
In the event of getting a part which falls under CID category, under mentioned process
would be followed:-
 
An e-mail communication from HO will be sent to respective Branch CSM, who in turn will
further inform their respective SERVICE PROVIDER  s with CID details & snapshot of the affected part.
 
Declaration of CID case:- From Warehouse, within 3 days of the material received date and
from Repair Centre- Dhaka, within 10 days from the material received date.
If SERVICE PROVIDER   desires, he can visit the Warehouse within 2 BDs of getting the intimation to
verify the defective part. Acer HO will wait for 3 BDs for the Branch to revert in case of any exception/
waiver that Branch CSM may want with the approval of RCSM. Once the confirmation is received,
thereafter a communication will be sent to Payout processing team for initiating the deduction.
Any query or clarification sought should be taken up with your respective RCSM.
The Penalty amount will be deducted (as per the Table given in the next page ) from the
current Partners claim/ payout & the details of the Partner invoice will be e-mailed to the
respective Branch for necessary reference.
In the event of pre-approved CID case, SERVICE PROVIDER   to put the e-mail approval printout along
with the affected part.

Declaration of RoHS Compliance for Veriton X2240G 
 
 
ACER India, subsidiary of ACER Inc. (“the Company”) hereby declares that ACER Veriton X2240G 
placed on International Standard are compliant with Directive 2011/65/EU on the Restriction of Certain 
Hazardous Substances in Electrical and Electronics Equipment. 
 
In order to protect the health and safety of users and reduce the potential risks of products to the 
environment, ACER carefully manages chemical substances in raw materials, and 100% of our main 
products are compliant with RoHS and product safety-related regulations. 
 
Since the European Union' (EU) promulgation of the RoHS directive, Acer has been monitoring its 
amendments and new process to ensure the compliance of the requirements. From 3rd January 2013, RoHS 
recast (RoHS 2) Directive 2011/65/EU enlarges the product scope and introduces the conformity process 
in harmonization with CE marking methodology. And in 2015, EU commission publishes Directive (EU) 
2015/863 to amend Annex II to EU RoHS 2 (Directive 2011/65/EU) to add 4 phthalates (DEHP, BBP, DBP 
and DIBP) onto the list of restricted substances from 22 July 2019 and our products are RoHS compliant. 
 
The product herewith complies with the requirements of the Radio Equipment Directive 2014/53/EU, the 
council EMC directive 2014/30/EU and the RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU. 
 
This declaration represents the Company’s knowledge and belief which is partially based on information 
provided by third party suppliers.
